title: Welcome
text:
Welcome to R Fundamentals for Health Research — an introduction to R for epidemiology and health policy research.
These materials
These course materials run entirely in your browser using Quarto and WebR. You do not need R installed locally to explore the tutorials and run code on this site.
Try loading a course dataset:

title: Welcome
text:
How to use this site

Tutorials — Pre-class activities with interactive code cells. Click Run in any code block to execute R in your browser.
Homework — Assignments with editable code cells. Download the original .Rmd from the course repo if you prefer working in RStudio.
Final Project — End-of-quarter analysis assignment.







Note



The first time you run code on a page, WebR needs to download R and any required packages. This can take 30–60 seconds on a slow connection. Subsequent cells on the same page run much faster.

title: Welcome
text:
Working locally in RStudio
If you prefer the full RStudio experience, clone the course repository and open the .Rmd files directly. The .qmd files on this site are generated from those sources for WebR compatibility.
